..release-notes-body:
## Installation

### Gradle (Maven Central) — via BOM

```kotlin
repositories {
    mavenCentral()
}

dependencies {
    // Import the BOM — all module versions are managed automatically
    implementation(platform("com.smellouk.kamper:bom:{{version}}"))
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:engine")
    // Optional metric modules (no version needed when using BOM):
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:cpu-module")
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:fps-module")
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:memory-module")
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:network-module")
}
```

### Gradle (Maven Central) — without BOM

```kotlin
dependencies {
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:engine:{{version}}")
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:cpu-module:{{version}}")
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:fps-module:{{version}}")
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:memory-module:{{version}}")
    implementation("com.smellouk.kamper:network-module:{{version}}")
}
```

### CocoaPods (iOS via XCFramework)

```ruby
pod 'Kamper', '~> {{version}}'
```

Then run `pod install`.

### Direct XCFramework download

[Kamper.xcframework.zip](https://github.com/smellouk/kamper/releases/download/v{{version}}/Kamper.xcframework.zip)

libs/ui/rn.release-notes-body:
## Installation

### npm

```bash
npm install react-native-kamper@{{version}}
```

or with yarn:

```bash
yarn add react-native-kamper@{{version}}
```

### iOS (CocoaPods)

Run `pod install` from your `ios/` directory after installing the package. The native iOS bridge is bundled with the package.

### Android

The native Android bridge is bundled with the package via the React Native autolinking mechanism — no manual `MainApplication.kt` changes required.

### React Native New Architecture (TurboModule)

`react-native-kamper` requires React Native ≥ 0.73 with the New Architecture enabled. See the package README for the imperative API (`Kamper.start/stop/on`) and the hooks API (`useCpu`, `useFps`, `useMemory`, `useNetwork`).
